Name: -1_to_and_for_the
Representative_Docs: ["As someone who works for the university an answer that comes to mind is to pay people more, particularly people who are in lower paying jobs. I'm doing well but not everyone employed here is making a wage that would create security. If I had children or was caring for a family member inflation would have me on edge right now and I would perhaps need to earn more to feel secure. ", "Right now we are doing a good job starting up the food pantry for students, and working on a community garden.  I know that some of my co-workers either work or have had to work multiple jobs in order to live sustainably, so having more conversations about that and coming up with solutions would be good.  While having good food security at home is the best option, I think having a cafeteria with consistent hours and reasonable food prices could potentially be helpful for our student and staff population.  As for housing, we don't offer anything that I am aware of to address that issue.  We can only point others to outside resources that are available.", 'The pay is insufficient even for faculty who have been here many years.  Students, who we encourage to get an education, see people with 10 + years of university and PhDs earning less than a manager at a Starbucks with a high school diploma.  They take note of this and basically think we are stupid to go into a profession that pays so little.  The college\'s view is that if anyone in teaching wants to earn good money, get out of teaching as fast as possible, and go into administration because that\'s where the money is.  But, what if teaching is our gift, and we want to share that, but also earn a decent salary--the college doesn\'t care, the Board and the Legislature doesn\'t have a clue, and they think administrators are more qualified (nothing could be further from the truth) or that they have more responsibility . . . but they are constantly saying that we, the faculty, are face-to-face with students and that\'s the most important thing, but obviously it isn\'t because they won\'t pay us what we\'re worth.  So many people are leaving this profession because of poor treatment.  NEVER say "people don\'t go into education for the money, it\'s a vocation" NO IT ISN\'T, it\'s a job, a job that requires years of graduate school.  We earn and deserve decent pay--we\'re not Mother Theresa, we\'re people who want to look after our families. ']

Name: 4_housing_campus_dorms_dorm
Representative_Docs: ['More campus housing', "Our motto is iCare which couldn't be further from the truth.  Administration tries to run the college like a business and only cares about the almighty $$$.  They are so anti-employee and anti-faculty which impacts the entire campus.  If they truly cared about our students, they would provide more food resources for our students and more assistance in other areas. When our students can't find housing, our student achievement center hands the student a piece of paper with numbers for apartment complexes.  If they cared, they would actually make phone calls, it would show they cared.  I also would like to see some temporary emergency housing offered for our students.", 'There is a serious housing shortage in town. The university has dorms for students and does make dorm rooms available to faculty and staff, but having a housing specialist to help people locate available housing and/or having university housing available for faculty/staff to buy or rent would make it possible for more people to work at the university. People routinely turn down job opportunities because they cannot find nearby housing.']

Name: 14_worry_about_my_bills
Representative_Docs: ['It is exhausting to constantly be worrying about costs. I tried to cut internet from home but it was required for work or classes. I make too much so I do not qualify for any help. I have to keep my home at freezing temps so that my gas bill is not too high, but then wake up with no water to take a shower. It is a horrible feeling to go to work unbathed. ', 'In one of the text boxes I explained this but I want to share it here so it is obvious. I\'m temporary part-time faculty but I\'m working OVER "full-time". I work more than a full-time employee (depending on the semester). This semester I\'m 108% employed. Despite this insane amount of work, I make very little, worry about saving for retirement, don\'t have any job security, don\'t have reliable benefits, and feel my only option to make this work is to live with my parents and eat their food.', 'I worry about my next meal or if I will have enough money to pay my rent and additional bills as well as gas.']

Name: 16_housing_rent_find_in
Representative_Docs: ["There is a large and well-supported literature empirically linking land use restrictions (zoning, height and density limits, architectural/style limitations, etc.) to more limited housing supply. The University could play a valuable role in disseminating this information. (For reference, please see Harvard's Edward Glaeser or GMU's Alain Bertaud).", "Rent for housing in Gallup is high; home prices are also expensive. I don't know if I would qualify for a mortgage out here even though I've previously owned 2 other homes. But if I did, the money we saved between the rent and the mortgage would go to student loans. I couldn't even use that $ to offset the costs of inflation/COLA.", "I am currently living in senior housing, but now that I'm working full time I will have to find new housing. Rents are so high I have been searching for a house, but can't find anything in my approval range that doesn't require major repairs I can't afford. I'm very worried about finding affordable housing when I have to leave here. Anxiety/depression when condos I thought I might be able to afford fell through because of huge repair costs. I normally do not have anxiety/depression."]

Name: 26_state_tuition_students_loans
Representative_Docs: ['I feel these issues need socioeconomic structural changes in society requiring legislation at the state and federal levels to provide people with basic needs and free tuition so that no one has to deal with being insecure in food and shelter, or has to choose between getting an education or buying food for their family. ', 'I Having been a student in the bad old days some of the issues with food was that I did not qualify for any assistance,  My undergraduate was from Texas A&M and they had a great program from former students that supplied loans that did not have interest to pay tuition which could be paid off at the end of semester.  There were enough jobs around that I could work several and pay it off.  Graduated without debt.  Here there are not enough jobs even though the city is much bigger and federal loans is the only way, especially for graduate students, to make ends meet.  The grad students at Texas had health insurance which eased my burden.  Of course, the state had approved such a thing.  ', "The prevalence of poverty and food insecurity in the state and DoÃ±a Ana County dramatically highlight the need for basic needs support for our students in higher education in the state. Our legislators, elected reps, and state dept. of higher ed. need to be made aware of this. Thank you, UNM for conducting this study to provide the data that will be critical to forcing state leadership's attention to the issue."]

Name: 68_transportation_bus_route_city
Representative_Docs: ["I rely on ABQ city busses to get to/from work on UNM main campus. Reliability of the bus line I take (790) has become increasingly unreliable, especially for the morning route. The bus is often late to pick us up, or won't show up at all. ", "I appreciate that this survey exists. I think the public transportation piece is really important. We were excited about the Blue Bus, but haven't heard any updates. Is that still happening? It would be great if we could have a UNM-Taos shuttle that goes into town. It would be great advertising for UNM-Taos and could maybe even increase enrollment. ", 'The city of Albuquerque has decreased bus services; they discontinued a route that would place me within walking distance of my home. I now must ride later bus and walk in the dark home at lease 1-2 times a week. The commuter routes feel safer than the Lomas route (example) I have had two scary rides with people who had outbursts and were threatening to the bus driver and other passengers. the city bus is much more focused on down-town workers with regular work hours and not UNM workers who have later and earlier working hours that are not consistent. I  have only one car and share with my spouse so that I can afford for my disabled son to have transportation; I pay for the least expensive UNM parking, but park in the lot only about once a week. It is expensive parking for just once a week.']

Name: 74_santa_fe_ineligible_spend
Representative_Docs: ['(1) Pay faculty a living wage for Santa Fe.  (2) Allow faculty who teach online courses to be remote; there is absolutely NO NEED to spend resources to come to campus for online students.  I doubt administration would spend resources so unwisely.', "I am only staff (full-time), but it was very demoralizing to find that my level of pay, after many years of working here, makes me ineligible for the vast majority of the rental units in Santa Fe.  Ineligible/unaffordable depending on the landlord's or the renter's perspective.  Ineligible based on the percentage of rent to gross income.", 'Housing costs are too high for wages in NM.  Renting or owning...a one bedroom apartment in an average neighborhood costing $1400 or a mortgage costing 1400.  My take home pay does not allow for me to spend that much.  I rent from a friend of the family and they discounted the rent so I could stay there long term.  I do not have a vehicle payment.  I do not travel nor spend much on entertainment.  NM has never been good to me about wages versus cost of living.  I have always struggled in this state.  I am a graduate of NMSU and I have a masters degree.  I hope this helps.  I would like to ask the State of NM to think about adding the Roadrunner train to Socorro or a charter bus.  We currently use van pools and car pools.  I would like to ride the train.']
